title: Samson
anchor_summary: Open-world action game built around close-quarters brawling plus aggressive car handling/vehicular takedowns, with mission-driven city traversal, player progression, and pressure from a reactive crime/police system.

similar_games.title: Mad Max
similar_games.why_similar: Open-world roaming centered on brutal melee combat, vehicle combat/chases, upgrades, and using cars as core weapons rather than mere transport.

similar_games.title: Sleeping Dogs
similar_games.why_similar: Urban open-world crime structure with hand-to-hand combat, driving-heavy mission flow, and story-driven progression through hostile city districts.

similar_games.title: Grand Theft Auto IV
similar_games.why_similar: Single-player open-world crime game with mission-based city progression, driving as a constant verb, police heat, and pressure from criminal obligations.

similar_games.title: Batman: Arkham Knight
similar_games.why_similar: Blends free-roam city traversal, close-quarters combat, upgrade paths, and a heavily integrated combat-capable vehicle in mission design.

similar_games.title: Mafia III
similar_games.why_similar: Story-forward open-world crime loop with district control, driving between jobs, escalating law/enemy response, and progression through violent missions.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently present Samson as a single-player open-world action game centered on close-quarters brawler combat and weaponized driving inside city districts. The strongest recurring gameplay signals are open-world districts, jobs/missions with consequences, climbing and city traversal, brawler combat, car chases/collisions, and a skill tree. That supports open_world_action_adventure more strongly than racer archetypes, which appear over-weighted from store genre labels. Racing/driving is important, but the copy frames cars as one combat/traversal tool within a broader urban action-adventure structure rather than the sole core loop.
